channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Rodri to have back surgery but Maresca brushes off transfer speculation
item.description: World Cup winner Rodri is to have back surgery, says new Manchester City boss Enzo Maresca.

item.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
item.description: Jurgen Klopp starts his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy.

item.title: IOC says Infantino complaint outside its scope
item.description: The IOC says it will not investigate Gianni Infantino as a complaint about the Fifa president is "outside" its scope.

item.title: Guardiola turns down Italy manager job
item.description: Former Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola turns down the chance to coach Italy.

item.title: Messiah or papering over cracks? Klopp must sway doubters in Germany rebuild
item.description: Jurgen Klopp is a legend at his former clubs, but he will need to convince his critics if he is to succeed in his toughest managerial challenge yet.

item.title: Chelsea agree deal to sign Palace defender Lacroix
item.description: Chelsea agree deal to sign France defender Maxence Lacroix from Crystal Palace after two impressive seasons.

item.title: Newcastle seal £34m move for Monaco's Bamba
item.description: Newcastle United have signed Monaco midfielder Aladji Bamba for a fee which could rise to £34m.

item.title: Senegal appeal date set after Afcon win overturned
item.description: The Court of Arbitration for Sport sets a date for a hearing over Senegal's appeal against being stripped of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title.

item.title: Scottish sides in Europe postpone league matches
item.description: All five Scottish teams in European competitions have opted to postpone their Premiership matches scheduled for Saturday, 22 August.

item.title: Kane set for talks with Bayern Munich over new deal
item.description: Harry Kane is set to begin talks over a new Bayern Munich contract, with Barcelona and various Saudi Arabian clubs reportedly interested in the England captain.

item.title: 'I'm proud of it' - Keegan looks back on career highlights and legacy
item.description: Kevin Keegan talks to Football Focus' Dan Walker in 2018 about some of the standout moments from his career - from winning World Player of the Year twice, to managing England and the iconic "I would love it" remark from the 1995–96 season.

item.title: The defining moments of the 2026 World Cup
item.description: From historic World Cup debuts and emotional first victories, to new stars and the brilliance of legends - relive the defining moments of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Keegan's double inspires Liverpool to 1974 FA Cup glory
item.description: Kevin Keegan scores twice as Liverpool defeat Newcastle United 3-0 in the 1974 FA Cup final.

item.title: Watch the BBC's closing World Cup montage
item.description: Watch the closing montage of the BBC's coverage of the 2026 Fifa World Cup featuring all the highs, lows and colour from the biggest World Cup ever.

item.title: Argentina boss Scaloni leaves press conference in tears
item.description: Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni leaves his post-match news conference in tears when talking about his future after Sunday's World Cup final defeat by Spain.

item.title: Why Rogers and Palmer will thrive together in Alonso's Chelsea
item.description: Why Morgan Rogers' skillset means he and fellow attacking midfielder Cole Palmer will prosper in Xabi Alonso's Chelsea.

item.title: Van Bommel appointed Belgium head coach
item.description: Belgium appoint former Netherlands captain Mark van Bommel as their new head coach on a contract running through Euro 2028.

item.title: Palace want teenage Brest defender Le Guen
item.description: Crystal Palace are interested in signing 19-year-old Brest centre-back Raphael le Guen.

item.title: Australia star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
item.description: Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato allegedly tests positive for cocaine after being twice caught for speeding in Sydney.

item.title: Are Celtic fans right to be nervous now?
item.description: Former Celtic striker Cillian Sheridan suggests the Scottish champions are making the same mistakes as last season, so we look at how their preparations are going.

item.title: What's working - and what's not - for WSL fans?
item.description: BBC Sport spoke to fans of the Women's Super League and England Lionesses to learn about their experiences in the game and discuss what could be improved.

item.title: Man Utd mascot, six, hit by club's ticket tout crackdown
item.description: Ruari's mum denies any wrongdoing and says she manages other family members' tickets for them.

item.title: Who am I? Guess Premier League star No 4
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: England amputee team's World Cup dream on hold
item.description: Former head coach Scott Rogers has said it is "criminal" that England men's amputee football team could miss the 2026 World Cup due to a lack of funding.

item.title: Devlin opens up on 'too stressful' Hearts exit for Rangers
item.description: Cammy Devlin says it was "too stressful" to head to the World Cup with his club future still to be decided - although the extra time taken did lead to a move to Rangers.

item.title: Seven stars to watch at Wafcon 2026 and why
item.description: As the biggest ever Women's Africa Cup of Nations gets ready for kick-off, BBC Sport Africa picks seven players to keep an eye on in Morocco.

item.title: Scotland's Gunn concedes four in San Jose debut defeat
item.description: Scotland goalkeeper Angus Gunn suffers a nightmare debut for San Jose Earthquakes as they slump to a 4-0 defeat at home to Orlando City in their first match following Major League Soccer's break for the World Cup.

item.title: Hearts sign Mendy for potential Forest Green record fee
item.description: Forest Green Rovers sell midfielder Laurent Mendy to Heart of Midlothian for what the National League outfit say could be a club record fee of nearly £1m "if all targets are met".

item.title: Former Brighton keeper Baggaley joins Birmingham
item.description: Newly-promoted Birmingham City sign goalkeeper Sophie Baggaley as a free agent after she leaves Brighton.

item.title: 'A date with history and we got there first' - Spain react to World Cup win
item.description: Spain manager Luis de la Fuente and his players react to winning their second World Cup title after beating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the final.

item.title: Dominant Spain beat Argentina to win World Cup after extra time
item.description: Ferran Torres scores the only goal of the game as Spain dominate Argentina 1-0, to win their second FIFA World Cup at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

item.title: 'The reaction is terrible' - World Cup final turns ugly at full-time
item.description: The frustration of some Argentina players boils over at the end of the 2026 World Cup final, after the 2022 winners are beaten by Spain in extra time.

item.title: Mbappe wins race for the 2026 World Cup golden boot
item.description: All the goals from the race for the 2026 World Cup golden boot as Kylian Mbappe leads the way from Lionel Messi, Jude Bellingham and Erling Haaland.

item.title: Spain's Torres scores only goal of World Cup final
item.description: Ferran Torres breaks the deadlock, scoring in extra time for Spain in the World Cup final against Argentina at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

item.title: Stars shine at the World Cup final half-time show
item.description: Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, and Shakira entertain the crowd at half-time in the World Cup final, with a special appearance from Ronaldo and Ronaldinho at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

item.title: England beat France in 10-goal thriller to win third-place play-off
item.description: Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick as England beat France 6-4 in a thriller to record their best men's World Cup finish since 1966.

item.title: Brilliant first half, turbulent second - Tuchel
item.description: England manager praises his players as England beat France 6-4 for third place in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Newcastle and Liverpool plan Keegan tribute at season opener
item.description: Newcastle United and Liverpool are in discussions over a tribute to Kevin Keegan before their Premier League opener at St James' Park on Sunday 23rd August.

item.title: Modric, 40, signs new AC Milan deal
item.description: AC Milan midfielder Luka Modric - who turns 41 in September - signs a one-year contract extension at the club.

item.title: Man Utd bring in Leicester goalkeeper Leitzig
item.description: Manchester United sign goalkeeper Janina Leitzig from Leicester City for an undisclosed fee.

item.title: Arsenal set to make £70m bid for Newcastle's Guimaraes
item.description: Arsenal are set to make a £70m offer for Newcastle captain Bruno Guimaraes.

item.title: Leeds in talks to sign Man City keeper Trafford
item.description: Leeds United are in discussions with Manchester City to sign goalkeeper James Trafford.

item.title: Can Emery work his magic again at Villa?
item.description: Aston Villa are back in the Champions League but must now cope with the loss of key players Morgan Rogers and Youri Tielemans.

item.title: Man Utd handed Tchouameni boost - Friday's gossip
item.description: Real Madrid open to selling Aurelien Tchouameni to Manchester United, Arsenal eye John Stones and Ezri Konsa, Chelsea favourites for Kerim Alajbegovic, Yan Diomande back on radar of Premier League clubs and Daizen Maeda set for Ipswich Town move.

item.title: What could Burnham as PM mean for football?
item.description: Andy Burnham is only into his second day as the UK's Prime Minister, but already people are wondering what this could mean for sport.

item.title: Garnacho, Rogers and two clubs trying to balance the books
item.description: How can Chelsea afford Morgan Rogers? And why are Aston Villa pursuing a loan for Alejandro Garnacho rather than buying him?

item.title: The country that doesn't play football... yet
item.description: Nauru is the only nation to have never played an official international football match and hardly anyone there plays the game. Is that about to change?

item.title: Gifted, charismatic and passionate amid highs and lows - Keegan obituary
item.description: The positivity and vibrancy Kevin Keegan brought made him a cherished figure within the game, writes chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: How to follow the new football season on BBC Sport
item.description: When the World Cup ends we'll still have loads of great content every day as we build up to the new football season.

item.title: All done deals in July 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Does World Cup third place play-off count towards Golden Boot?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the history of World Cup Golden Boot winners and the third-place play-off

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: What are the key dates for the 2026-27 football season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything explains the full list of key dates for the 2026-27 men's football season

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Who needs what in Premier Sports Cup?
item.description: With one round of fixtures left in the Premier Sports Cup group stage, who needs what to progress this weekend?

item.title: Can Hibs recover from calamity in Kosovo?
item.description: Hibernian’s hopes of reaching the Conference League are in jeopardy after a shock 2-0 defeat by Malisheva, with head coach David Gray saying his team "were way below the levels required".

item.title: 'Celtic close on Hogh' as Maeda & Danilo near exits - gossip
item.description: Bodo/Glimt's Kasper Hogh is reportedly close to joining Celtic with Daizen Maeda poised to complete his switch to Ipswich Town and Rangers' Danilo pictured arriving in Brazil to complete a loan move.

item.title: Codina on why she joined West Ham project
item.description: Laia Codina won trophies and found a "new family" during her three-year spell at Arsenal - but she was ready for a change with West Ham.

item.title: Goalkeeper Startup joins WSL newcomers Birmingham
item.description: Birmingham City have signed goalkeeper Katie Startup following her exit from Manchester City when her contract expired.

item.title: Arsenal sign Spain goalkeeper Rodriguez on free
item.description: Arsenal bring in Spain goalkeeper Misa Rodriguez on a free transfer - the Women's Super League side's sixth signing of the summer.

item.title: Newcastle closing in on Liverpool forward Kiernan
item.description: Newcastle United are in advanced talks to sign Republic of Ireland forward Leanne Kiernan from Liverpool.

item.title: In spotlight but shunning limelight - Olise's unique rise to top
item.description: A footballer who dislikes too much media attention, but Michael Olise might not have much choice if he carries on his World Cup heroics.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.
